item.title: My craft: I design and make clothing for people with physical differences and disabilities.
item.description:
This delightfully Hobbity waistcoat is designed for people with fine motor impairments and digestive disorders. It looks like wool but it is a stretchy fleece and can be taken off over the head. It is sewn together at the buttonholes so that it moves and gives like it is actually buttoned closed. Because the vest is stretchy, it can change with your stomach needs over the course of the day. Did you know that some of us have stomach disorders that cause our waist sizes to change by 4 in in the course of just one hour? That can make it so that clothing that zips up becomes an agonizing prison very quickly. This skirt is a very twirly full circle skirt with a hidden placket in the back that allows you to adjust it to whatever your waist size is in the moment. This is great for people who have stomach cancer, ulcers, diverticulitis, ibs, and crohn's. It's also great for people recovering from eating disorders who need clothing that doesn't force them to check in with their weight everyday.
This is a shot of the back placket that allows for the adjustability. Different skirt through. This beauty is fully lined wool.
This is a full circle skirt and it features a different kind of adjustable back. The back is made with extra fabric that can be gathered into a bunch or pleats to give the effect of a Victorian walking skirt. Because I'm all about that drama.
This dress is made out of double brush knit which is one of the softest fabrics known to man. It has cute little puff sleeves, but it's also nice and stretchy so that it can move with your stomach, and is very gentle on the sensory needs. It's also a pullover, making it ideal for people with fine motor difficulties.
I started designing clothing for my stomach disorder out of necessity. I started doing it professionally when I realized how many people could benefit from adaptive clothing, but either had no idea it existed, or were turned off by how depressingly bland the options were. Why do the designers of adaptive clothing think that disabled people want to dress like boring white people in Olive garden commercials? We want personality, verve, expression!
Each item is made to your custom measurements. Are you at 6'5 and you've never actually had a floor-length skirt touch the floor? I can help. Are you 4 ft 6 and never had a maxi dress that didn't try to swallow you whole? I've got you. This dress is a pullover and made out of sensory friendly flannel. It's great for people with stomach difficulties because it is cut in the swing style, so you can belt it for a fitted look or wear it loose for maximum comfort. Belted styles are also great for people recovering from eating disorders because you just pull it on, tie your belt and go.
Overalls are the most comfortable clothing on Earth and I don't understand why we don't all wear them all the time.
This is my pinafore apron worn over one of the other dresses. Because everything I make has to have massive pockets.
This pinafore dress is my favorite cottage core look. It is very comfortable with its adjustable waistband in the back and flat waistband in the front and oh so cute. I specifically designed cottage core, goblin core, and dark academia clothing so that people with disabilities can have exciting options. If you like what you see, feel free to follow me on Facebook and Instagram @eightfoldfox and check out my website at www.eightfoldfox.com.
item.dc:creator: EightfoldFox

item.dc:creator: aFadingMemory

item.title: Just ugh.
item.description: So I've been having a really nice time getting to know this young lady, and things seemed to be going quite well. But then the word policing began... I said "things are kinda crazy at work." Apparently, crazy is a bad word because it's offensive to the neurodivergent, and I'm not allowed to use it regardless of the context having nothing to do with it being used as a slight against anyone. I said "I appreciate a partner who is intelligent and capable." Apparently, capable is a bad word because it's offensive to the disabled, and I'm not allowed to use it regardless of the context having nothing to do with it being used as a slight against anyone. There were more like this, and it just got to the point where it's so exhausting trying to police completely innocent words that I just can't be fucking bothered dealing with this person. I understand that certain words can be harmful, and I try my best to be considerate and thoughtful, but for fucks sakes, relax, lady. Yeesh. * Update, since so many folks were asking for one. I tried to reach out and talk to her about it this morning and it turns out she has me muted and blocked on the app where we met, as well as texts. Not very satisfying for y'all I know, but there's nothing much I can do about it.
item.dc:creator: HomeDepotAisle15

item.title: She needs a new set of chompers
item.dc:creator: Az663

item.title: $200 for a service call to fix my dishwasher? I think NOT.
item.description: Yesterday I put my dishwasher through a self-clean cycle & opened it to find the bottom was filled with water. Called around & turns out 1) repair-people *hate* fixing Bosch dishwashers & 2) the one place that *would* fix it wanted to charge $200 for it - I bought it used for less than that 3 years ago! My wife & I settled on buying a new one but I kept digging online because a new dishwasher is $$$ & then I pulled off the bottom panel & looked inside. I put the new part in my cart & pulled the old one free only to find the drain pump was jammed by butcher’s twine which must have been stuck in the bottom of a Tupperware or something. Cleaned the spindle & put it back in & it’s FIXED! For free! Very proud of myself!
item.dc:creator: StevenAlleyn

item.title: IMGUR NEEDS AN OPSEC BRIEF.
item.description: Do not post pics about troop movements, locations, dates, numbers of troops and equpt. PLEASE Russia and the enemy can look and see details in photos and conversations that put ukrainias and freedom fighters in danger. They can get geolocations, meta data, facial recognition, and info from photos that you may have never considered. Your pic alone might not be enough but the enemy has lots of info and can use your pic to solve a puzzle. You might think "sauce" on a cool pic or story is nice but so does the enemy. You might think a simple pic of a "tank" or group of guys without other info is harmless but the enemy can see other photos and comments and piece things together. ALSO IT DOESNT MATTER IF THE PIC IS OLD OR FROM ANOTHER SITE, ITS STILL SPREADING VALUABLE INFO THE ENEMY CAN FIND AND USE. DONT LET IMGUR HELP RUSSIA AND HURT OUR FORCES. Please stop helping Russia guys! Ask yourself before posting... could this help putin find/hunt/kill/or workaround ukrainian forces?
item.dc:creator: icutyouinyoursleep

item.title: Taking an overnight train from Chicago to New York
item.description:
Story time! I was in Chicago today for a business trip with my favorite partners when my buddy tells me there’s a “bomb cyclone” winter storm coming to the area and our flights back east the next day might be delayed. Terrible thoughts of missing the weekend with my family cross my mind until another friend suggests a sleeper car on Amtrak. Turns out there an overnight train from Chicago to New York that takes a dozen hours, and after checking the Amtrak website, I found a nice option - a one bedroom compartment with private bathroom and shower in your suite! Who knew!
It was expensive ($1200!) but I could get a refund on my hotel room and flight and actually make it home instead of being stuck waiting for canceled flights or, worse, driving through a winter storm even airplanes won’t fly through. So I pulled the trigger and visions of the orient express danced in my head while sharing my excitement with my business partners over dinner. I had to leave dinner early to make sure I was on time for the train, but the restaurant was kind enough to wrap up my dinner to go so I could enjoy it in my one bedroom suite before bed. Here are some little known facts about my trip:
- A one-bedroom compartment with an in-suite bathroom isn’t all it’s cracked up to be. I’m sure the residual moisture on the floor was cleaning solution. But could also be toilet water. I’m not going to investigate further.
- It was definitely a bad idea to bring my leftover dinner onto the train with me. No mini fridge. Duh. I guess I wasn’t thinking but now my room smells like steak. I think it will be a different smell in the morning after sitting at room temperature all night.
- The train was warmer than I thought it would be on a cold March night in Chicago. I found a thermostat thingie but I learned the dial is about as helpful as pushing an elevator button 7 times when it’s already lit. (Probably just there to make you feel better. )
- Before going to bed, it’s fun to check under your the seats for freebies from the last guest. I won a free pair of used underwear playing that game!
- The bench seat turns into a bed. Nice feature. Turns out the upper bunk is hidden away and released with a lever, so when you turn it, the top bunk comes down and reveals a 3” mattress already adorned with clean sheets and pillows. You just take the mattress/sheet combo and put it on the flattened bench seat and - presto! - a nice bed. More comfortable than it sounds, actually. A solid 5/7.
- One little gripe, perhaps: if you’re taller than 5’9”, your feet will touch the wall when laying down. I am now wondering how many other feet I’m now foot-cousins with. I decided to wear my socks to bed.
- I’m pretty sure the people in the one bedroom suite next to mine have gas. Either that or the leftovers are starting to turn. There’s a helpful 1” gap under the door between our rooms so I can’t tell which. - I think there’s a 40% chance my locked compartment door will open on its own due to the train motion at some point during the night. And because I didn’t pack pajamas, I can’t help but think someone is going to be tapping my shoulder while I’m sleeping half naked and darth vadering through my CPAP mask, jumpstarting my consciousness like a taser on a sheep’s rear-end. Should make for interesting dreams. Will I make it back to my home state? I’ll update tomorrow with the latest. —— Morning update! https://imgur.com/gallery/GmNYl00
item.dc:creator: jeffv2718

item.title: Mental Health
item.description: US Suicide Prevention Call or text: 800-273-8255 or 988 More info or chat online: https://suicidepreventionlifeline.org/ American Foundation for Suicide Prevention Www.afsp.org Great information & resources regarding depression (has been really beneficial to me): https://thedepressionproject.com/ Anxiety and Depression Association of America: https://adaa.org/understanding-anxiety Informational post by @Eindride, @DrivebyPianist Imgur people to talk to, resources, etc : http://imgur.com/gallery/xZiVRmo Need a safe place to be open with your mental health? Join Discord, Need to talk: https://discord.gg/gWDbZCbErQ Wholesome Discord run by @ryanjoseph666 https://discord.gg/FUbc4r5zFk
item.dc:creator: Scrumptiousassumptions
